title: pow: transport.rs mine PoW in connect flow
description:
Wire PoW mining into core/src/transport.rs ws_upgrade_and_auth.

What: After receiving ChallengeParams, extract server_salt and difficulty; mine PoW in blocking thread; add pow_token to AuthenticateParams.

Algorithm in ws_upgrade_and_auth():
1. After receiving Challenge notification and extracting params (ChallengeParams),
   decode params.server_salt from base64 to Vec<u8>; on error return Err
   let difficulty = params.difficulty;
2. If difficulty == 0 (PoW disabled): pow_token = None
3. If difficulty > 0:
   a. Get pub_key_bytes from identity.verifying_key().to_bytes()
   b. Compute ts_floor = SystemTime::now().duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH).unwrap().as_secs() / 60 as u32
   c. Clone pub_key_bytes, server_salt_bytes for move into blocking thread
   d. let token_str = tokio::task::spawn_blocking(move || {
         nie_core::pow::mine_token(&pub_key_bytes, &server_salt_bytes, difficulty, ts_floor)
      }).await.expect('spawn_blocking never fails');
   e. pow_token = Some(token_str)
4. Construct AuthenticateParams with pow_token field set
5. Continue with signing and sending as before

Note: mine_token is CPU-bound. spawn_blocking offloads to Tokio's blocking thread pool.
At diff=20, expect ~1M SHA256 doublings (~100-300ms on modern hardware). This is acceptable.

Defensive programming:
- server_salt decode failure returns transport::Error (not unwrap)
- spawn_blocking await unwrap is safe: task only fails on panic, and mine_token does not panic
- ts_floor as u32: truncation is safe for many decades; use explicit cast with comment
- If relay sends difficulty > 30: mine_token will return a valid token with diff=30 cap? No — mine_token should just iterate normally; the relay's verify_token will reject tokens where token.diff > 30. The client should mine what the relay asks. If relay sends absurd difficulty, client mines forever. Document: relay is trusted for difficulty value (it's not an adversarial protocol).

Note: pub_key_bytes is available from sign_challenge() return: it returns (pub_key_b64, sig_b64); decode pub_key_b64 back to bytes for PoW; OR expose identity.verifying_key().to_bytes() from the identity type. Check which is cleaner.
acceptance_criteria: cargo test -p nie-core passes; transport.rs calls mine_token when challenge.difficulty > 0; pow_token set in AuthenticateParams
close_reason: transport.rs: ws_upgrade_and_auth now mines PoW when challenge.difficulty > 0; pow_token included in AuthenticateParams
